data.body:
G4 child-consistency review (parent 3fe1 + this CLI-surface sweep child; whole sibling roster considered). One high-confidence cross-child finding (conflicting assumptions / consumer-impact mis-attribution of the load-bearing reconciler `list --full` contract). The core sweep scope ‚Äî KEEP stdout data prints in `_cli`/`_commands`, migrate stderr diagnostics, bring broad-excepts to the floor, scoped-T201 allow, remove from BLE001 allowlist ‚Äî is otherwise consistent and well-scoped (verified in-tree).

Verified facts (read_file / search_files):
- `_cli/` + `_commands/` are the WRITE-command surface: the dispatcher (`_cli/__init__.py`) routes transition/claim/reopen/compact/delete/fsck/txn/etc. into `_commands/*`, and these carry the real diagnostics + data prints this child legitimately owns ‚Äî e.g. `_commands/composer.py` (~17 `print(..., file=sys.stderr)` diagnostics), `_commands/{claim,transition,delete,session_log,show_config,fsck}.py` (`print(json.dumps(...))` stdout data prints), and 20+ broad `except Exception` sites across `_commands/` plus 3 in `_cli/`. So the child's "classify stdout DATA vs stderr DIAGNOSTIC; keep data, migrate diagnostics; floor the excepts; scoped-T201 allow" is real, correctly scoped, and non-overlapping with sibling sweeps. (`file=sys.stderr` count in `_cli/` itself is 0; all CLI-surface diagnostics live in `_commands/`.)

---
FINDING 1 ‚Äî Conflicting assumptions / consumer impact: the child claims ownership of the load-bearing reconciler `rebar list --full` stdout-JSON contract, but that contract's actual `print(json.dumps(...))` site lives in `_engine_support/` (sibling d360), NOT in this child's `_cli`/`_commands` scope.

- finding: This child's description opens by asserting "[t]he CLI surface is where stdout `print(json.dumps(...))` data output legitimately lives ‚Äî that is a machine contract and MUST stay on stdout (the reconciler `json.loads` it)", and AC1 reads "Stdout data `print(json.dumps(...))` output in `_cli/`/`_commands/` is unchanged (contract intact)." But the specific reconciler-consumed contract ‚Äî `rebar list --full`, which `reconcile.py:466` does `json.loads(result.stdout)` on after running `[cli, "list", "--full"]` (reconcile.py:453) ‚Äî is dispatched (via `_cli/__init__.py`'s read routing) to `_engine_support/reads_cli.py`, whose `list` arm emits the stdout JSON at `reads_cli.py:336` (`print(json.dumps(states, ...))`). `_engine_support/` is sibling d360's file_impact, not this child's. So the child's framing attaches the reconciler `json.loads` contract to a package it does not touch.
- criteria: [this child description "the reconciler `json.loads` it"; this child AC1 "Stdout data `print(json.dumps(...))` output in `_cli/`/`_commands/` is unchanged (contract intact)"; parent "stdout is sacred ... the reconciler `json.loads` it at reconcile.py:472"; parent SC2 "stdout stays pure (CLI data contract ... intact)"]
- location: src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py:453 (`[str(cli), "list", "--full"]`), :466 (`json.loads(result.stdout)`); src/rebar/_engine_support/reads_cli.py:336 (`print(json.dumps(states, ...))` ‚Äî the `list` arm's stdout data print); src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py:264-286 (`_FIELD_READS`/`_LOOKUPS` route reads into `_engine_support`, NOT `_commands`); d360 file_impact {grounding/, _engine_support/}.
- evidence:
  - reconcile.py:453/466 runs the ticket CLI `list --full` subprocess and `json.loads` its stdout ‚Äî the exact contract the parent + this child cite.
  - The `list` subcommand is NOT handled in `_cli`/`_commands`: `_cli/__init__.py` routes reads (`list`/`show`/`search`/`ready`/`deps`) to `_engine_support` (reads_cli/lookups), while `_commands/*` are the write arms (transition/claim/delete/compact/txn/...). search for `def _list`/`list_states` in `_cli` ‚Üí 0 hits; the `list` data print is `reads_cli.py:336`.
  - `_engine_support/` is in d360's file_impact and explicitly described by d360 as the sweep that touches it; it is NOT in this child's file_impact ({_cli/, _commands/}).
  - Cross-confirmation: d360's own G4 review independently flags that `_engine_support/reads_cli.py` carries ~14 stdout `print(json.dumps(...))` data sites (incl. the `list` arm) and that 8fbd's purity test only guards `rebar list --full` ‚Äî i.e. the `list --full` contract is owned/at-risk in d360's slice, not here.
- scenarios:
  - An implementer reading this child's "keep stdout data prints / contract intact (the reconciler json.loads it)" looks only in `_cli`/`_commands`, finds the WRITE-command data prints (claim/transition/delete/...), preserves those, and reasonably concludes "the reconciler contract is safe in my packages." But the reconciler-consumed `list --full` print is in d360's `_engine_support/reads_cli.py:336` ‚Äî so neither this child nor a reader of it actually guards the cited contract; the guard responsibility silently sits with d360 (which classifies `_engine_support` as library/core, the very mis-scope d360's G4 flags).
  - The parent's stdout-purity contract test is owned by 8fbd ("`rebar list --full` stdout parses as JSON"); that test exercises d360's `_engine_support` `list` arm, not this child's `_commands` write prints ‚Äî so this child's AC1 "contract intact" is, for the *specific reconciler contract it names*, neither implemented nor tested within its own scope.
- impact: A cross-child attribution gap: this child's description + AC1 take credit for preserving the reconciler `json.loads(list --full)` machine contract, but that contract's data-print site and its purity guard both live outside its scope (d360 + 8fbd). The genuine risk to that contract is in d360's `_engine_support` sweep (where d360's G4 already flags a library/core-vs-CLI mis-classification of the same stdout prints). This pairing's framing can lull both an implementer and a reviewer into believing the reconciler contract is covered "here" when it is not ‚Äî a high-confidence consumer-impact/conflicting-assumptions mode for THIS pairing.
- checklist_item: conflicting assumptions; consumer impact
- suggested_fix: Re-scope the description/AC1 wording to the data prints this child actually owns (the `_commands/*` write-command `print(json.dumps(...))` outputs ‚Äî claim/transition/delete/session_log/show_config/fsck), and explicitly hand off the reconciler `rebar list --full` contract to its true owners: the data-print site is `_engine_support/reads_cli.py:336` (sibling d360) and the purity guard is sibling 8fbd's `list --full` test. Either drop the "the reconciler json.loads it" claim from THIS child or add a cross-reference noting that the reconciler-consumed read contract is owned by d360/8fbd, so the contract is provably guarded by an owning ticket rather than implied here.

---
Modes checked and CLEAN (high-confidence, benign-reading filter):
- Implicit shared state (BLE001/T201 per-file allowlist): benign ‚Äî this child removes only its OWN packages (`_cli`/`_commands`) from the allowlist authored by 16a1 and emptied by 0ec1; sequenced via blocks edges (16a1 ‚Üí fd72 ‚Üí 0ec1), no concurrent writer.
- Scope overlap: benign/none ‚Äî `_cli`/`_commands` are uniquely this child's; no other sweep lists them in file_impact (8d01 = __init__/reducer/graph/_store; d360 = grounding/_engine_support; ceaa = mcp_server/llm; 0569 = rebar_reconciler; 16a1 = llm/plan_review). The read-vs-write split means the symmetric `_engine_support` CLI-presentation surface is d360's, not a double-owned region. (Note: `_io/_cli.py` exists and is dispatched from `_cli/__init__.py:235-242`, but it lives under `_io/` ‚Äî outside this child's `_cli`/`_commands` file_impact ‚Äî so it is not a scope overlap here; it is the separately-noted un-owned `_io/` package, already filed against 16a1/0ec1, not re-attributed to this pairing.)
- Ordering violation: benign ‚Äî correctly sits after substrate 8fbd and after 16a1 enables BLE001/scoped-T201 (8fbd ‚Üí 16a1 ‚Üí fd72), and blocks the 0ec1 close-out; the named logger this child migrates diagnostics into exists before this sweep runs.
- Dependency gap: benign for THIS pairing ‚Äî the un-owned `_io/` package is a real roster hole but is outside this child's stated `_cli`/`_commands` scope and is already filed against 16a1 (allowlist author) and 0ec1 (empty-allowlist gate); not re-attributed here per the absence cross-check.
- Residual references: benign ‚Äî the only drift is the shared stale cite `reconcile.py:472` for the `json.loads(stdout)` contract (actual call is reconcile.py:466); below the high-confidence/benign-reading bar (points at the same load-bearing call). The deeper substance of that contract's mis-attribution is captured as FINDING 1 above.

The one finding above is the conflicting-assumptions/consumer-impact mode: this child's description claims the reconciler `list --full` stdout contract while that contract's print site (`_engine_support/reads_cli.py:336`) and purity guard (8fbd) live in sibling scope (d360/8fbd), not in `_cli`/`_commands`.
